summary_text: **Recommended Boundaries** - `Notion` = editorial control plane and UI only. Editors create/approve content, set status, and adjust copy. - `Analytics DB (Supabase/Postgres)` = source of truth for quoted-author history, diversity metrics, ingestion events, and review outcomes. - `x-auto runtime queue` = execution buffer for posting (ephemeral/operational), never the long-term analytics store. - `Object/log storage` = raw ingestion artifacts (source snapshots, parser output, audit logs). **Recommended End-to-End Flow** 1. **Ingestion** - Inputs: `Source URLs`, manual quote seeds, optional feed crawlers. - Normalize each source and create immutable `source_event`. - Extract quoted-author candidates and compute stable `author_id` (canonical URL/handle/name fingerprint). - Write all extraction records to Analytics DB with provenance and confidence. 2. **Editorial Review (Notion UI)** - Create/refresh Notion draft rows with minimal fields: `Body`, `Body JA`, `Source URLs`, `quoted_author_display`, `quoted_author_id`, `confidence`, `status`. - Editors decide: accept author mapping, edit copy, reject quote, request re-ingestion. - Only editorially relevant fields live in Notion; no heavy metric history. 3. **Sync Contract (Notion ↔ Analytics)** - Notion → Analytics: approved/rejected decisions, final selected author, publish intent. - Analytics → Notion: derived badges only (e.g., “author overused”, “domain repeated”, “diversity risk”). - Conflict rule: editorial fields follow Notion; analytic/event fields are append-only in Analytics DB. 4. **Diversity Scoring** - Compute per-candidate `diversity_score` before approval and again before scheduling. - Include at least: author concentration (recent N posts), source-domain concentration, topic repetition, language/pattern repetition. - Enforce gates: hard block for extreme repetition, soft warning for moderate repetition. 5. **Operational Use (Future)** - Scheduler uses diversity score as a tie-breaker when multiple approved drafts exist. - Weekly planning uses author/domain coverage reports from Analytics DB. - Build “underrepresented author/topic” recommendations for next draft generation. **Operational Policies** - **Fail-closed on missing provenance**: no publish path without `quoted_author_id` and source evidence. - **Immutable event log**: ingestion/review/sync actions are append-only with actor + timestamp. - **Deterministic identity policy**: author merge/split operations must be explicit and audited. - **Separation of concerns**: Notion never becomes analytics warehouse; Analytics DB never edits editorial copy. - **Two-stage gating**: editorial approval gate, then diversity/safety gate at schedule time. - **Replayability**: every posted row must trace back to ingestion event, reviewer decision, and scoring snapshot. **Minimal Schema Recommendation** - `author_registry(author_id, canonical_name, handles, canonical_url, active)` - `quote_sources(source_id, url, fetched_at, checksum, extractor_version)` - `quote_candidates(candidate_id, source_id, author_id, snippet, confidence, topic_tags)` - `editorial_decisions(decision_id, notion_page_id, candidate_id, decision, reviewer, decided_at)` - `diversity_snapshots(snapshot_id, notion_page_id, score, factors_json, computed_at)` - `publish_outcomes(outcome_id, notion_page_id, tweet_id, posted_at, runtime_run_id)` If you want, I can turn this into a concrete implementation contract (`docs/agents/x-auto-quoted-author-contract.md`) plus test checklist next.
